text.title: Four-File Memory Split, Append-Only, Cited
text.summary: Memory lives in four committed Markdown files; entries are append-only and each carries a citation back to its origin.

section.title: The four roles and the append-only contract
section.p: Memory is institutional knowledge the agent re-loads on every session start. Splitting it by role lets the agent fetch only the relevant slice and lets reviewers focus on one shape of change at a time.

rule.statement: Every project using agent-memory MUST keep exactly four files at `.aidocs/memory/` (or `.product/memory/` per per-project convention): `decisions.md`, `patterns.md`, `mistakes.md`, `session.md`. Each entry MUST be a Markdown section starting with a date in `YYYY-MM-DD` form, a one-line title, and at least one citation — a commit SHA, ticket URL, PR URL, or transcript hash. LLM agents MUST append; they MUST NOT edit or delete prior entries. A pre-commit hook MUST reject any diff to `.aidocs/memory/*.md` (except `session.md`) that removes lines, and MUST require at least one citation token (`commit:`, `ticket:`, `pr:`, or `transcript:`) per added entry.
rule.rationale: Append-only turns memory into an audit log: any future agent or reviewer can trace why a belief exists. Citations forbid hallucinated lore — every claim points to a verifiable source. The split prevents one of the four roles from cannibalising the others (the typical failure mode where `mistakes.md` becomes a dumping ground for every observation).

example.code: ## 2026-04-26 — pin uv 0.4 floor **Decision.** Repo standardises on `uv >= 0.4`; CI installs from the lockfile only. **Why.** uv 0.3 lockfile format is incompatible; mixed envs caused 3 build breaks. **Citation.** commit:9f3ac1a · ticket:NERO-412
example.note: Dated, titled, cited, single role (decision). Future agents trust it because it points back to the originating commit and ticket.

example.code: ## Notes - Probably should use uv now. - Don't forget the staging-DB password. - That auth bug we fixed once — try not to break it again.
example.note: No dates, no citations, mixed roles. Two months later no one can tell what is current, what was disproven, or what file would even own it.

antipattern.description: Letting an LLM "consolidate" or "summarise" the memory files automatically.
antipattern.reason: The summary discards citations and rewrites prior entries with the model's current biases; the audit trail evaporates and reviewers cannot tell what was added vs invented.

section.reference: Skeleton with the required header schema.
GitHub Engineering — opaque agent memory as a review-coverage gap.
